Petition Of Elizabeth Rabkin For Reimbursement Of Attorneys Fees And Costs From Trust
Off Calendar Sep-22-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky appearing by CourtCall for Elizabeth Rabkin. Emilie Calhoun appearing by Zoom for Nancy Rabkin (by Zoom) and John Rabkin (by Zoom). Emilie Calhoun was also appearing for Lawrence Rabkin who did not appear. Sheila Robello appearing by Zoom for Robert Rabkin. Charles Rabkin appearing in pro per by Zoom.

The Court stated that there was an order by Judge Quinn for a meet and confer. Ms. Calhoun stated that there wasn't a meet and confer. Ms. Calhoun also stated that the trust didn't require court supervision, that there were six co-trustees, that it didn't request formal accountings only exchanging of documents/information, that there was a claim that Charles Rabkin collected rent money and kept it, that the efforts made were to save money, that it wasn't successful, and that a formal accounting was filed but it did not bring anything new. The Court addressed Ms. Calhoun stating that her client's took a position, that they hired a lawyer, and inquired as to why the trust should pay. Ms. Calhoun provided a brief response. Mr. Zabronsky addressed his pending motion to be relieved as counsel, stated that their position is that all the siblings/trustees/beneficiaries were in a heated controversy, that each is there to protect their own positions, that all parties are fighting each other, that each party should cover their own fees, that the trust should not cover the fees, that all individuals are out to protect their own interets at varying degrees, and that the fees should be borne by each. The Court stated that the motion to be relieved as counnsel is on calendar 12/7/20 and that it is reluctant to continue the petitions. Ms. Calhoun stated that Judge Quinn said that he would take each one at a time but he didn't say which one first. Ms. Robello stated that Ms. Calhoun misstates this case, that there wasn't transparency, that this admisitration is been going on for twelve years, that there is one property that needs to be sold, that the accounting did show assets, that Charles Rabkin had been taking funds for years, that Robert Rabkin's fees should be granted, and that there still are assets outstanding. Ms. Robello stated that the Court determined that there was too much to be heard in one setting. Charles Rabkin objected to any further delays, stated that all should be borne by each individual, that no attorney's fees or costs should be paid, and noted that he is not going to be reimbursed for his expenses. The Court denied the request for a continuance. Ms. Calhoun argued that Ms. Robello was incorrect, that the parties stipulated to an accounting, that the Court didn't order an accounting, and that there was a "break out" re: attorney's fees for the Court's ease. Brief additional argument by Mr. Zabronsky is held. The request for an evidentiary hearing was denied. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the the Petition for Instruction To: 1) Reimburse From the Trust Attorney's Fees and Costs Incurred by Petitioner Co-Trustees, Rabkin, Lawrence Rabkin, and John Rabkin, for Benefit of the Trust; etc. and the Petition of Elizabeth Rabkin for Reimbursement of Attorneys Fees and Costs from Trust under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition Of Beneficiary And Cotrustee For Removal Of Cotrustees And For Appointment Of Debra Dolch As Successor Trustee And Related Orders
Off Calendar Oct-22-2018 Continued To Mar-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Wolff for and with Elizabeth Rabkin (Co-Trustee/Beneficiary).
Franchesca Callejo and Natalie Lupo for and with Robert Rabkin (Co-Trustee/Beneficiary).
Emilie Calhoun for and with Nancy Rabkin, John Rabkin and Lawrence Rabkin (by telephone).
Charles Rabkin appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the third account is under submission. Ms. Calhoun stated that the fourth account is with Martin Harris, that she should have it in a couple of weeks, and that she submitted a status conference statement, but it did not go through. Discussion was held with regard to the removal of Robert, Robert needing to sign documents, sale of the computer shares, the Court ordering all Beneficiaries to sign documents to implement the decision of the majority of the Co-Trustees, concern about Robert having signature power at the bank, the holdings assets and the proceeds from the condo having been distributed to the beneficiaries, two offers for the Tahoe property from two of the Co-Trustees, some of the beneficiaries still using the Tahoe property, the parties not wanting to do confirmation of sale and not wanting to deal with public overbids, etc. The Court asks if the parties have tried mediation. Ms. Callejo stated that they need a referral to the trial department and that the trial would be more than two half days. The parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss the matter. The matter was recalled. All parties were present. The Petition for Removal of Co-Trustees, etc. is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on March 4, 2019 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court/a copy of the order and notice of trial was provided to Counsel). The Ex Parte Petition for Suspension of Co-Trustees, etc. is off calendar. Status on filing of the fourth account is set for December 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reporter: Mary Visciglio, CSR #10391, 415-362-4346.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Continued To Sep-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo and Kevin Lachona for and with Robert Rabkin (Petitioner/Co-Trustee).
David King and Emilie Calhoun for and with John Rabkin and Nancy Rabkin, and for Lawrence Rabkin (Petitioners).
Charles Wolff for and with Elizabeth Rabkin.
Charles Rabkin in Propria Persona.

Ms. Calhoun stated that there is an issue with regard to how much rent Charles took, how much he is to return and how much he is to keep. Ms. Callejo stated that there are dividends and escheated assets, that without access to the records it is premature to have the accounting approved, and requested that the Court not grant the accounting and allow time for review of the records. Discussion was held with regard to the ten year delay in administration, the lack of cooperation between the Co-Trustees, lack of transparency, hostility between the Co-Trustees, the need to have a professional appointed, delay in sale/purchase by one of the Trustees of the Tahoe property, past history and behavior of the Co-Trustees, language in the Trust with regard to the majority making decisions, etc. The Court orders a ten minute recess for Counsel to have discussion with their Clients to narrow down what the issues are and how they can be resolved. Upon returning, Mr. Lachona stated that he would like to see his Client being added to all of the accounts, that he would like a list of all of the accounts and who is on title, and he would like to have his Client receive statements for all of the accounts. Mr. King stated that he agrees to the request, that the condominium is in escrow, that John is going to purchase the Tahoe property, that the escheated assets require all of six Trustees to sign for recovery of the funds and that there are $800,000.00 of assets that need to be transferred from Dorothy Rabkin to the Trust. Ms. Calhoun stated that they agreed to the next accounting period of October 1, 2016 through December of 2017. Charles Rabkin stated that he collected $49,849.00 in rents and he is charging $47,000.00 for managing the property. The Court orders Mr. King to provide documents to Mr. Lachona, stated that the Tahoe property should be sold at market value. that it will not allow the Parties to use it as the Chairperson and that if the parties do not agree to anything, they should go to the American Arbitration Association. The Petition for Removal of Co-Trustees and the Ex Parte Petition for Suspension of Co-Trustees are continued to October 22, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status. The accounting petition is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reporter: Beth E. Grant, CSR #10943, 707-888-3689.

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for and with Robert Rabkin.
Emilie Calhoun for and with Nancy Rabkin and for Larry Rabkin and John Rabkin.
Charles Wolff for and with Elizabeth Rabkin.
Charles Rabkin in Propria Persona.

Ms. Calhoun addressed the issue of notice stating that this isn't a challenge to the Trust, that this is an accounting in Dorothy Rabkin's Trust, that the GST Trusts are the beneficiary and that all of the Children are Trustees in the GST Trusts. Ms. Calhoun also stated that the subtrusts are the beneficiaries, that the subtrusts have been funded, that there are no contingent beneficiaries of Dorothy's Trust, that this is not an accounting of the GST Trusts, that it is an accounting of the parent trust and explained the reason as to why they preferred not to give notice to the grandchildren. Counsel reported that the only remaining issues are Charles Rabkin's collection of rents for the Maryland property that have not been accounted for, the tangible personal property (what is in the boxes packed by Robert in 2006) and what was in the safe deposit box when emptied out in 2004. Ms. Calhoun asked the Court to do the same as in the first accounting where Charles is allowed to keep half of the rents collected for his work and be required to pay the other half back to the Trust. Charles Rabkin stated that the invoices he submitted are not computed in the accounting, but the charges to him are, that the second accounting does not start with an amount and that he is at a disadvantage. The Court stated that it is requiring 30-day notice to all parties, that is setting status on filing of the third accounting and setting a trial date. Mr. Koplowitz stated that the third accounting is already prepared and made it clear that no one has requested a trial or an evidentiary hearing. The Second Report and Account, etc. is continued to December 15, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any additional filings are to filed by December 8, 2017. All Counsel are to provide Ms. Calhoun with current addresses of all of the children and grandchildren. Counsel prefer to receive a status hearing date on the third accounting at the next hearing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Chu. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Continued To Sep-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David King for (petitioners) John Rabkin, Nancy Rabkin & Charles Rabkin who are present in court and Lawrence Rabkin. Robert McWhorter for (co-trustee and beneficiary) Robert Rabkin who is present in court. Thomas Latham for (co-trustee) Elizabeth Rabkin who is present in court.

Mr. King asked if the court required a copy of the will and codicils, stated that he has provided copies to counsel and was instructed to file the documents prior to presenting them to the court. Mr. McWhorter stated that they are not in opposition to the Heggstad petition, that they object to some parts of the order, that they have issue with the Gorlin-Rabkin partnership and that there are accounting issues to be addressed in the next petition. Mr. Latham expressed his support of the Heggstad petition. Mr. King reported that issues were raised early on regarding the number of shares, that everyone was in agreement regarding Gorlin-Rabkin and that Elizabeth concurs that it is not part of the estate. The Court asked if the parties were amenable to mediation in terms of the underlying petition. Mr. King stated that they would like to avoid further litigation, that the 6 beneficiaries are the 6 trustees, that there is no reason they cannot work together, that Lawrence has 10,000 pages of documents, that he has offered that anyone can review them and select documents to be copied, that Robert has participated since his mother's death and has access to most if not everything, that the cost of a forensic accounting for the last 6 years would be about $100,000.00 and that they would like to hone in on the areas of concern. The court asked the parties if there was anything short of a formal accounting that would work. Mr. McWhorter stated that he sent a letter requesting a meeting, that there was no response except from Elizabeth, that Dr. Rabkin has had no access to information since 2009, that as a co-trustee he should have full access, that pages 18-19 of the petition lists categories of documents that they want access to, that they want access to the bank account information and that they want to know if the other beneficiaries have been paid. Mr. King stated that there had been transparency at one time, requests a list of what they want with an explanation and that all expenses are paid from one account. ***Mini-minutes continued in next entry for this date**

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David King for (petitioners) John Rabkin, Nancy Rabkin & Charles Rabkin who are present in court and Lawrence Rabkin. Robert McWhorter for (co-trustee and beneficiary) Robert Rabkin who is present in court. Thomas Latham for (co-trustee) Elizabeth Rabkin who is present in court.

***Mini-minutes continued from first entry***

Mr. McWhorter questioned the premise of the $100,000.00 cost of the accounting, stated that there is a duty to account, that the information should be readily available, that the request goes back to 2010, that they tried to submit a stipulation that identified documents to avoid a petition but counsel would not stipulate and that having an accountant in Maryland does not make sense with the administration in San Francisco. Mr. Latham stated that it would not cost much to have the 10,000 documents in Lawrence's posession scanned, that it would need to be done if there is an accounting and that everyone could have access to them. Mr. King stated that the issues should be worked out without an accounting, that there is no basis for the request, that as a co-trustee Robert has a duty as well and that they all agreed to waive accountings. Mr. McWhorter stated that the issue of the waivers was addressed in the reply, that there are no written waivers on file, that certain rights are not waiveable, that the trust mandates that annual accountings be produced, that it has not been complied with and that the only access they have is to the brokerage account. The court stated that as a first step the documents need to be scanned and produced to Dr. Rabkin and that the parties need to work out what is fair and reasonable and not beyond that. Mr. King stated that they are willing to consider the suggestion of scanning. Mr. McWhorter stated that they would also want to know if these are all of the documents or if other trustees have documents and requested a status conference to figure out if an accounting is necessary. Mr. King stated that they want the US Savings Bonds and jewelry that is not accounted for and that it appears that either Robert or Elizabeth has these items. The verified petition for access to financial records, etc., was continued to September 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Instructions was taken under submission. The parties are to scan and transmit the documents no later than July 17, 2015. The parties are to review the documents and draft status statements due no later than September 7, 2015. Mr. McWhorter is to prepare a proposed order.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
